source_or_gap: Pinned local CCA project source and unresolved cross-project provenance for durable semantic memory, compiler governance, benchmark truth, proof coupling, and self-modification mechanisms.
chapter_action_policy: Update existing chapters with bounded implementation-reference patterns after exact claim mapping; defer a new durable-semantic-memory chapter until BeastBrain primary provenance and overlap are reviewed.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge compiler, governance, benchmark, proof, mesh, and self-mod findings into their existing layer owners. Re-evaluate a split only for the durable semantic-memory substrate after BeastBrain mining.
boundary_decision_rationale: CCA is primarily a convergence implementation reference. Most mechanisms already have book owners; durable semantic state and learned lattice navigation may be a distinct substrate but primary lineage is not yet fully mined.
external_literature_need: knowledge-graph and memory-system comparators for a possible durable semantic-memory chapter
formal refinement and proof-to-code coupling comparators
proof_or_test_backlog: audit semantic depth of CCA Lean obligations and runtime mappings
replay one bounded benchmark-truth or proxy-trace artifact family if retained inputs permit
hash and classify repeated checkpoints before storage cleanup
evidence_transition_precondition: exact project passages or code surfaces mapped to each book claim
cross-project primary provenance and deduplication completed
project-reported results reproduced or explicitly retained as source-reported context
accepted Appendix C transition recorded before any support promotion
required_before_drafting: complete BeastBrain and MoECOT overlap review
keep raw private project material outside the public repository
inspect current chapter source queues before adding CCA mappings
preserve negative results and no-claim boundaries
promotion_blockers: expensive project result families not replayed
proof models are compact abstractions rather than full-system proofs
source-publication permission is not recorded for the raw project tree
residuals: durable semantic-memory chapter decision remains open
accepted versus diagnostic artifact families need a retained-input audit
checkpoint and Git-history duplication remain uncleaned
next_action: Deep-mine MoECOT, then compare CCA mechanisms to predecessor provenance before exact chapter and claim mappings are added.
non_claims: does not claim CCA capability, safety, corrigibility, efficiency, AGI, or ASI
does not reproduce project training, benchmark, proof-to-runtime, or deployment results
does not authorize deletion or Git history rewriting
does not promote an ASI Stack support state
